mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect rising aggregate wage income, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when PAYEMS increases month-over-month, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 1% signals highly accommodative monetary policy that reduces the equity discount rate and stimulates borrowing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or the mean 60-day return during triggered periods is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts reveal a broad contraction in consumer and residential investment demand, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND HOUST declines month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a bivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, accelerating industrial production alongside sticky inflation generates input cost pressures and tightens financial conditions, compressing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of late-cycle expansion observations when INDPRO is rising and CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month trailing average, or the conditional mean 60-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month contraction in real GDP signals declining aggregate demand that lowers corporate sales and profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when GDPC1 3-month growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates improving manufacturing efficiency and capacity utilization, which supports positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.5 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over three months signals a deteriorating labor market that undermines consumer confidence and spending, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated month-over-month inflation prints raise discount rates and compress real equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month, or the rank correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and 20-day returns is above -0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.
